body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

This cell is the *cap-hit* sibling of `quota-burndown`. Where `quota-burndown` asserts that `Windows[0].UsedPercent` rises monotonically *below* the cap, `token-quota-exhausted` is the distinct condition where a quota window has actually hit 100%. For Codex this is the v3 (Jan 2026+) `rate_limit_reached_type` field inside `event_msg.payload.rate_limits`. `extractCodexRateLimits()` in `core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/parser.go:360-361` reads it into `RateLimitSnapshot.ReachedType`, which flows through the metrics adapter (`core/adapters/outbound/metrics/adapter.go:218,269`) into the domain (`core/domain/session/rate_limit.go:31-33`). So the agent emits a quota-exhausted signal and irrlicht observes and stores it.

**Why partial, not yes:** `ReachedType` is surfaced as a snapshot field for the UI to render a warning state — there is no dedicated session lifecycle state for quota exhaustion. The three-state machine (working/waiting/ready) has no `quota-exhausted` state; a quota-capped turn still resolves to `ready`. The signal is a metric, not a state transition. So irrlicht observes the snapshot but does not model exhaustion as a first-class lifecycle event.

## Why this cell degrades to applicable:false at the implement stage

The observability is fine; the blocker is *deterministic elicitation*, exactly as the scenario's own description states for claudecode: "a real quota exhaustion cannot be induced deterministically against the live API."

1. You cannot burn the real Codex subscription/account quota on demand to make the live API return a `rate_limit_reached_type`.
2. There is no codex/OpenAI-compatible mock-server primitive analogous to the claudecode path. The claudecode recipe relies on a bespoke Anthropic-only stub (`tools/mock-anthropic-429.go`) plus `tools/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh`, which is hard-wired to launch `claude --bare` so it reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` from env. No equivalent stub or `--bare`-style env override exists for codex.
3. The codex interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-codex-interactive.sh`) has no primitive to inject a quota-exhausted (`rate_limit_reached_type`) response into the transcript.

With no way to inject the response and no way to burn real quota, the `ReachedType` signal cannot be elicited deterministically in a live `--attach` recording. The cell is therefore frozen as `applicable: false` with a scope_note that references `quota-burndown` (the in-window observability is already validated there) and the missing injection primitive. This is an elicitation gap, not an observability gap — if a codex 429/quota-injection mock (or a fixture-injection driver primitive) is ever added, the cell becomes recordable.

## What irrlicht does NOT do (and why that's OK)

irrlicht does not transition the session into a special state on quota exhaustion; the turn completes to `ready` and the exhaustion is visible only via the `ReachedType` metric snapshot. Modeling exhaustion as a lifecycle state is out of scope for the three-state machine.
caveats: Distinct from quota-burndown: burndown = sub-cap monotonic UsedPercent rise (already has a spec + assessment, no recording yet); this cell = the cap-hit ReachedType signal.

No deterministic live elicitation: real account quota cannot be burned on demand, there is no codex 429/quota mock (only the Anthropic-only tools/mock-anthropic-429.go), and the codex interactive driver has no quota-exhausted-response injection primitive.
Observed as a metric snapshot field (ReachedType), not as a lifecycle state transition — a quota-capped turn still resolves to ready.
Becomes recordable only if a codex/OpenAI-compatible 429 mock or a driver fixture-injection primitive is added (a developer task).

sources.note: v3 schema (Jan 2026+) adds rate_limit_reached_type; extractCodexRateLimits reads it into RateLimitSnapshot.ReachedType.
ReachedType: 'when non-empty, signals that one of the windows has hit its cap. UI uses this to switch the display into a warning state.' No lifecycle state.
ReachedType propagated through the metrics projection.
The claudecode recipe's 429-injection path is Anthropic-only and launches claude --bare; no codex equivalent exists.
Codex interactive driver step types: keys/wait_turn/sleep/interrupt/resume/reset_session/exit_clean — no quota-exhausted-response injection primitive.
Scenario description: 'a real quota exhaustion cannot be induced deterministically against the live API' — the same constraint applies to codex with no mock available.
